name: Rugelach (Cookie)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut cream cheese into 1-tablespoon pieces and let soften at room temperature.
2. Cut butter into small pieces of about 1/2 tablespoon and keep them cold until ready to use.
3. Place flour, salt, and butter in a bowl of food processor and process with on/off turns until mixture resembles coarse meal.
4. Add cream cheese and sour cream, distributing evenly over the mixture. Process with on/off turns until dough just holds together.
5. If necessary, add 1 teaspoon water. Wrap dough in plastic wrap, press together into a ball and flatten to a disk. Refrigerate for 4 hours or up to two days.
6. With the easy-to-handle cream cheese dough, crescent-shaped rugelach are delight to prepare at home. These are tender, flaky, and not too sweet. You can make them ahead and freeze before or after baking.
7. Roll out a portion of the dough in a circle about 1/16 inch thick. With a knife or pastry wheel, cut the pastry into 16 pie-shaped wedges. If the dough is sticky, dust it with a little flour.
8. Sprinkle or spread the filling on the wedge. Beginning at the wide edge, roll the dough up toward the point. Place on an ungreased cookie sheet and carefully sprinkle with a tiny bit of the reserved sugar.
9. Repeat with the remaining dough and filling. Bake for 15 to 18 minutes, or until golden.
